--- title: "Towards translational and mechanistic whole-brain simulations with The Virtual Brain" subtitle: "Track 4 Workshop — FAIR Brain Data Science Bootcamp" authors: - name: "Leon Stefanovski" - name: "Marius Pille" - name: "Leon Martin" affiliations: - id: 1 name: "Charité University Medicine Berlin · Berlin Institute of Health" date: 2026-05-12 format: clean-revealjs: slide-number: true scrollable: true auto-play-media: false chalkboard: true incremental: false toc: false logo: img/Charite.png footer: "© Pille, Martin, Stefanovski, Charité Unviersity Medicine Berlin" link-external-newwindow: true code-line-numbers: true highlight-style: github include-in-header: _slides-home-btn.html engine: jupyter jupyter: python3 --- ## Welcome {.unnumbered} - Two-day hands-on workshop on personalized whole-brain modeling with TVB - Day 1: structured sessions - Day 2: mini hackathon — bring your own questions / data - Useful links: - - - ## Schedule {.unnumbered} ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="60%" .smaller} **Day 1**\ **Block 1 - 09:00-12:00:**\ 1. Intro to Brain Network Modelling\ 2. Defining a FAIR brain network model\ 3. Dynamical perspective\ 4. Setup & First examples **Block 2 - 13:00-15:00:**\ 5. Bifurcation Theory\ 6. Parameter exploration & optimization **Block 3 - 15:30-17:30:**\ 7. Stimulating the brain ::: ::: {.column width="40%" .smaller} **Day 2**\ **Block 1 - 09:00-11:30:**\ Mini hackathon - Bring your own data / research question **Block 2 - 12:00-13:30:**\ Mini hackathon - Bring your own data / research question ::: ::: # 1. Intro to The Virtual Brain {background-color="#0b3d91"} - TODO: motivation for whole-brain network modeling - TODO: brief historical arc of TVB ## Basic ideas and history :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="25%"} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow1.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: {.column width="25%" .fragment} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow2.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: {.column width="25%" .fragment} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow3.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: {.column width="25%" .fragment} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow4.png){width="100%"} ::: :::: ## Mathematical framework $$ dS_i = \left[f_d(S_i, \theta^d, C_i, I_i) \right]dt + g(S_i, \theta^g)\, dW_i $$ ::: {.fragment} **State Evolution** - $S_i$ - state variables at node $i$ - $f_d$ - dynamics function with parameters $\theta^d$ - $C_i$ - coupling input from connected nodes - $I_i$ - external input (stimulation, driving signals) - $g$ - noise diffusion coefficient with parameters $\theta^g$ - $dW_i$ - Wiener process (stochastic fluctuations) ::: ## Dynamical Systems :::: {.columns} ::: {.column style="font-size: 0.55em;"} A mass $m$ on a spring with stiffness $k$.
Perpetual oscillation at $\omega = \sqrt{k/m}$. **State Equations:** $$ \dot{x} = v $$ $$ \dot{v} = - \frac{k}{m}*x $$ **State Variables** | **Variable** | **Initial Value** | **Unit** | **Description** | |--------------|-------------------|----------|-----------------| | $x$ | 2.0 | $\mathrm{m}$ | Displacement from equilibrium | | $v$ | 0.0 | $\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{s}}$ | Velocity | **Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Value** | **Unit** | **Description** | |---------------|-----------|----------|-----------------| | $k$ | 0.0001 | $\frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{m}}$ | Spring stiffness constant | | $m$ | 1.0 | $\mathrm{kg}$ | Mass of the oscillating body | ::: ::: {.column} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_single_thumb.png){fig-align="center"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_single.gif){.fragment fig-align="center"} ::: ::: :::: ## Initial Conditions {background-color="#ffffff"} :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="20%"} ::: {style="font-size: 0.8em;"} $$ \dot{x} = v $$ $$ \dot{v} = - \frac{k}{m}*x $$ ::: ::: ::: {.column width="80%"} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_ics_thumb.png){fig-align="center"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_ics.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" } ::: ::: :::: ## Phase Space {background-color="#ffffff"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/phase_space.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"} ## Parameters — Mass {background-color="#ffffff"} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_mass_thumb.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_mass.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"} ::: ## Towards Realism — Damping & Load {background-color="#ffffff"} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_realism_thumb.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_realism.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"} ::: ## Neural Mass Models - TODO: neural mass / mean-field formulation ## Network - TODO: nodes, coupling, delays, noise ::: {.fragment fragment-index=2} $$ C_i = f_c^{\text{post}}\Big(\sum_j A_{ij}\, f_c^{\text{pre}}(S_i, S_j(t-\tau_{ij}), \theta^c), S_i, \theta^c\Big) $$ ::: {style="font-size: 0.55em;"} **Coupling** - $f_c^{\text{pre}}$ - pre-aggregation transformation - $A_{ij}$ - structural connectivity weight ($j \to i$) - $\tau_{ij}$ - transmission delay (tract length / conduction speed) - $f_c^{\text{post}}$ - post-aggregation transformation - $\theta^c$ - coupling parameters ::: ::: ## Applications in basic research - TODO: representative findings - TODO: links to [@Schirner2023; @Koller2024; @Kashyap2025] ## Clinical applications - TODO: translational use-cases - TODO: virtual epileptic / Alzheimer / stroke patient examples # 2. Dynamical perspective {background-color="#0b3d91"} ## How to analyze a brain system - TODO: fixed points, limit cycles, bifurcations ## 3.1 Bifurcation analysis - TODO: 1D / 2D bifurcation diagrams - TODO: interpretation in the brain-network context # 4.
